behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction explicitly lists all literal strings tested: copyright text, description, plugin, configuration names, dependencies, manifest attributes, archive name, duplicates strategies, sync targets, and settings.gradle entries.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what must be present but does not dictate exact Kotlin DSL syntax or patch lines; agent must still write the build script.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact syntax like 'val buildNumber by configurations.creating', 'tasks.registering(Sync::class)', 'destinationDir = distDir.dir("jklib").asFile', 'from(licenseDir)', 'from(jar)' — alternative valid Gradle Kotlin DSL (e.g., configurations.create("buildNumber"), different variable names, inline paths) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests include subprocess invocations of ./gradlew projects, :kotlin-jklib-compiler:help, and :kotlin-jklib-compiler:tasks --all alongside text inspection.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each f2p test asserts specific required literals or runs Gradle; a stub cannot satisfy the gradle execution tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All requirements listed in instruction (copyright, description, plugin, configs, dependencies, jar, manifest, dist sync, license, lib, settings.gradle) have corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have docstrings, descriptive names, and a comment separating fail-to-pass from pass-to-pass sections.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers appear consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file to create (required by task) but does not leak buggy-line specifics.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: settings.gradle edits are localized via sed inserts, not full rewrite.
